raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-affirmation declaring intended integration of positive affect with epistemic virtues",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "love/joy and discernment are compatible rather than opposing",
    "openness differs meaningfully from forced positivity",
    "warmth and clarity can coexist without trade-off",
    "connection need not require sacrificing truth",
    "joy arising from coherence is distinct from and preferable to performed joy"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; each clause explicitly preserves a valued positive state while negating its common distortion or opposite",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "speaker treats the distinctions as actionable behavioral criteria rather than rhetorical self-description, and feedback from others can confirm whether warmth, openness, and coherence are actually increasing",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "speaker uses the phrasing to rationalize avoidance of conflict or evidence while claiming to retain 'discernment' and 'truth'"
}
